chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: So most of you can probably relate to what I'm feeling right now.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: My heart is racing in my chest.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: My palms are a little bit clammy.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: I'm sweating.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: And my breath is a little bit shallow.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Now, these familiar sensations are obviously the reason

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: why I'm feeling so good.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: I'm feeling so good.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: I'm feeling so good.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: I'm feeling so good.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: I'm feeling so good.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: I'm feeling so good.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: I'm feeling so good.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: I'm feeling so good.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: These sensations are obviously the result of standing up

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: in front of a thousand of you

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: and giving a talk that might be streamed online

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: to perhaps a million more.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: But the physical sensations I'm experiencing right now

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: are actually the result of a much more basic mind-body mechanism.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: My nervous system is sending a flood of hormones,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: like cortisol and adrenaline, into my bloodstream.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: It's a very old and very necessary response

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: that sends blood and oxygen to the organs and muscles

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: that we need to respond quickly to a potential threat.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: But there's a problem with this response,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: and that is that it can get over-activated.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: If I face these kinds of stressors on a daily basis,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: particularly over an extended period of time,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: my system can get overloaded.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: So basically, if this response happens infrequently,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: super necessary for my well-being and survival.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: But if it happens too much, it can actually make me sick.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: There's a growing body of research examining the relationship

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: between chronic stress and illness.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Things like heart disease and even cancer

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: are being shown to have a relationship to stress.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: And that's because over time, too much activation from stress

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: can interfere with my body's processes that keep me healthy.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Now let's imagine for a moment that I was pregnant.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: What might this kind of stress,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: particularly over the length of my pregnancy,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: what kind of impact might that have on the health of my developing fetus?

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Now you probably won't be surprised when I tell you

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: that this kind of stress during pregnancy is not good.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: It can even cause the body to initiate labor too early,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: because in a basic sense, the stress communicates

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: that the womb is no longer a safe place for the child.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Stress during pregnancy is linked with things like high blood pressure

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: and low infant birth weight.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: And it can begin a cascade of health challenges

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: that make birth much more dangerous for both parent and child.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Now of course, stress, particularly in our modern lifestyle,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: is a somewhat universal experience, right?

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Maybe you've never stood up to give a TED Talk,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: but you've faced a big presentation at work,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: a sudden job loss, a big test,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: a heated conflict with a family member or friend.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: But it turns out that the kind of stress we experience

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: and whether we're able to stay in a relaxed state long enough

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: to keep our bodies working properly depends a lot on who we are.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: There's also a growing body of research

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: examining that showing that people who experience more discrimination

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: are more likely to have poor health.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Even the threat of discrimination,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: like worrying you might be stopped by police while driving your car,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: can have a negative impact on your health.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Harvard professor Dr. David Williams,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: the person who pioneered the tools that have proven these linkages,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: says that the more marginalized groups in our society

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: experience more discrimination and more impacts on their health.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: So I've been interested in these issues for over a decade.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: When I became interested in maternal health,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: when a failed pre-med trajectory instead sent me down a path

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: looking for other ways to help pregnant people.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: I became a doula, a layperson trained to provide support

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: to people during pregnancy and childbirth,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: and because I'm Latina and a Spanish speaker,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: in my first volunteer doula gig at a public hospital in North Carolina,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: I saw clearly how race and class

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: impacted the experiences of the women that I supported.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: If we take a look at the statistics about the rates of illness

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: during pregnancy and childbirth,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: we see clearly the pattern outlined by Dr. Williams.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: African-American women in particular

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: have an entirely different experience than white women

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: when it comes to whether their babies are born healthy.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: In certain parts of the country, particularly the Deep South,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: the rates of mother and infant death for black women

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: actually approximate those rates in sub-Saharan Africa.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: In those same communities, the rates for white women are near zero.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Even nationally, black women are four times more likely to die

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: during pregnancy and childbirth than white women.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Four times more likely to die.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: They're also twice as likely for their infants to die

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: before the first year of life than white infants,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: and two to three times more likely to give birth too early or too skinny,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: a sign of insufficient development.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Native women are also more likely to have higher rates

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: of these problems than white women, as are some groups of Latinas.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: For the last decade as a doula-turned-journalist and blogger,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: I've been trying to raise the alarm

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: about just how different the experiences of women of color,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: but particularly black women,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: are when it comes to pregnancy and birth in the US.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: But when I tell people about these appalling statistics,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: I'm usually met with an assumption

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: that it's about either poverty or lack of access to care.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: But it turns out neither of these things tell the whole story.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Even middle-class black women still have much worse outcomes

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: than their middle-class white counterparts.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: The gap actually widens among this group.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: And while access to care is definitely still a problem,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: even women of color who receive the recommended prenatal care

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: still suffer from these high rates.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: And so we come back to the path from discrimination to stress to poor health,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: and it begins to paint a picture that many people of color know to be true.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Racism is actually making us sick.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Still sound like a stretch?

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Consider this.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Immigrants, particularly black and Latina immigrants,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: actually have better health when they first arrive in the United States.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: But the longer they stay in this country, the worse their health becomes.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: People like me, born in the United States to Cuban immigrant parents,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: are actually more likely to have worse health than my grandparents did.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: It's what researchers call the immigrant paradox,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: and it further illustrates that there's something in the US environment

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: that is making us sick.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: But here's the thing.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: This problem that racism is making people of color,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: but especially black women and babies sick, is vast.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: I could spend all of my time with you talking about it,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: but I won't because I want to make sure to tell you about one solution.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: And the good news is, it's a solution that isn't particularly expensive,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: doesn't require any fancy drug treatments or new technologies.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: The solution is called the JJ way.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Meet Jenny Joseph.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: She's a midwife in the Orlando, Florida,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: who's been serving pregnant women for over a decade.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: In what she calls her easy access clinics,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Jenny and her team provide prenatal care to over 600 women per year.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Her clients, most of whom are black, Haitian and Latina,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: deliver at the local hospital.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: But by providing accessible and respectful prenatal care,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Jenny has achieved something remarkable.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Almost all of her clients give birth to healthy, full-term babies.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Her method is deceptively simple.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Jenny says that all of her appointments start at the front desk.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Every member of her team and every moment a woman is at her clinic

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: is as supportive as possible.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: No one is turned away due to lack of funds.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: The JJ way is to make the finances work, no matter what the hurdles.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: No one is chastised for showing up late to their appointments.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: No one is talked down to or belittled.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Jenny's waiting room feels more like your aunt's living room

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: than a clinic.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: She calls this space a classroom in disguise.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: With the plush chairs arranged in a circle,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: women wait for their appointments in one-on-one chats with a staff educator

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: or in group prenatal classes.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: When you finally are called back to your appointment,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: you're greeted by Alexis or Trina, two of Jenny's medical assistants.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Both are young, African-American and moms themselves.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Their approach is casual and friendly.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: During one visit, I was asked to tell Jenny

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: that I was going to be a mother.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: During one visit, I observed Trina chatted with a young, soon-to-be mom

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: while she took her blood pressure.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: This Latina mom was having trouble keeping food down due to nausea.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: As Trina deflated the blood pressure cuff, she said,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: "'We'll see about changing your prescription, OK?

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: We can't have you not eating.'"

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: That we is actually a really crucial aspect of Jenny's model.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: She sees her staff as part of a team

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: that has one goal, get mom to term with a healthy baby.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Jenny says that Trina and Alexis are actually the center of her care model

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: and that her role as a provider is just to support their work.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Trina spends a lot of her day on her cell phone,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: texting with clients about all sorts of things.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: One woman texted to ask if a medication that she was prescribed at the hospital

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: was OK to take while pregnant.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: The answer was no.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Another woman texted with pictures of an infant born under Jenny's care.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Lastly, when you finally are called back to see the provider,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: you've already taken your own weight in the waiting room

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: and done your own pee test in the bathroom.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: This is a big departure from the traditional medical model

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: because it places responsibility and information back in the woman's hands.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: So rather than a medical setting where you might be chastised

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: for not keeping up with provider recommendations,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: the kind of settings often available to low-income women,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Jenny's model is to be as supportive as possible.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: And that support provides a crucial buffer

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: to the stress of racism and discrimination facing these women every day.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: But here's the best thing about Jenny's model.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: It's been incredibly successful.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Remember those statistics I told you,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: that black women are more likely to give birth too early,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: to give birth to low-birth weight babies,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: to even die due to complications of pregnancy and childbirth?

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Well, the JJ way has almost entirely eliminated those problems,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: starting with what Jenny calls skinny babies.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: She's been able to get almost all her clients to term

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: with healthy, chunky babies like this one.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: This is an infant, a baby girl, born to a client of Jenny's this past June.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: So a similar demographic of women in Jenny's area

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: who gave birth at the same hospital her clients did

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: were three times more likely to give birth to a baby below a healthy weight.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Jenny is making headway into what has been seen for decades

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: as an almost intractable problem.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: So some of you might be thinking,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: all this one-on-one attention that the JJ way requires

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: must be too expensive to scale.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Well, you'd be wrong.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: The visit with the provider is not the center of Jenny's model,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: and for good reason.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Those visits are expensive, and in order to maintain her model,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: she's got to see a lot of clients to cover costs.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: But Jenny doesn't have to spend a ton of time with each woman

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: if all of the members of her team can provide the support,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: information and care that her clients need.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: The beauty of Jenny's model is that she actually believes

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: it can be implemented in pretty much any health care setting.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: It's a revolution in care just waiting to happen.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: So these problems I've been sharing with you are big.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: They come from long histories of racism,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: classism, a society based on race and class stratification.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: They involve elaborate physiological mechanisms meant to protect us

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: that, when overstimulated, actually make us sick.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: But if there's one thing I've learned from my work as a doula,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: it's that a little bit of unconditional support

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: can go a really long way.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: History has shown that people are incredibly resilient,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: and while we can't eradicate racism

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: or the stress that results from it overnight,

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: we might just be able to create environments

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: that provide a buffer to what people of color experience

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: on a daily basis.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: And during pregnancy, that buffer can be an incredible tool

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: towards shifting the impact of racism on generations to come.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Thank you.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Thank you very much.

chunks.title: How_racism_harms_pregnant_women_and_what_can_help
chunks.text: Thank you.

text: So most of you can probably relate to what I'm feeling right now. My heart is racing in my chest. My palms are a little bit clammy. I'm sweating. And my breath is a little bit shallow. Now, these familiar sensations are obviously the reason why I'm feeling so good. I'm feeling so good. I'm feeling so good. I'm feeling so good. I'm feeling so good. I'm feeling so good. I'm feeling so good. I'm feeling so good. These sensations are obviously the result of standing up in front of a thousand of you and giving a talk that might be streamed online to perhaps a million more. But the physical sensations I'm experiencing right now are actually the result of a much more basic mind-body mechanism. My nervous system is sending a flood of hormones, like cortisol and adrenaline, into my bloodstream. It's a very old and very necessary response that sends blood and oxygen to the organs and muscles that we need to respond quickly to a potential threat. But there's a problem with this response, and that is that it can get over-activated. If I face these kinds of stressors on a daily basis, particularly over an extended period of time, my system can get overloaded. So basically, if this response happens infrequently, super necessary for my well-being and survival. But if it happens too much, it can actually make me sick. There's a growing body of research examining the relationship between chronic stress and illness. Things like heart disease and even cancer are being shown to have a relationship to stress. And that's because over time, too much activation from stress can interfere with my body's processes that keep me healthy. Now let's imagine for a moment that I was pregnant. What might this kind of stress, particularly over the length of my pregnancy, what kind of impact might that have on the health of my developing fetus? Now you probably won't be surprised when I tell you that this kind of stress during pregnancy is not good. It can even cause the body to initiate labor too early, because in a basic sense, the stress communicates that the womb is no longer a safe place for the child. Stress during pregnancy is linked with things like high blood pressure and low infant birth weight. And it can begin a cascade of health challenges that make birth much more dangerous for both parent and child. Now of course, stress, particularly in our modern lifestyle, is a somewhat universal experience, right? Maybe you've never stood up to give a TED Talk, but you've faced a big presentation at work, a sudden job loss, a big test, a heated conflict with a family member or friend. But it turns out that the kind of stress we experience and whether we're able to stay in a relaxed state long enough to keep our bodies working properly depends a lot on who we are. There's also a growing body of research examining that showing that people who experience more discrimination are more likely to have poor health. Even the threat of discrimination, like worrying you might be stopped by police while driving your car, can have a negative impact on your health. Harvard professor Dr. David Williams, the person who pioneered the tools that have proven these linkages, says that the more marginalized groups in our society experience more discrimination and more impacts on their health. So I've been interested in these issues for over a decade. When I became interested in maternal health, when a failed pre-med trajectory instead sent me down a path looking for other ways to help pregnant people. I became a doula, a layperson trained to provide support to people during pregnancy and childbirth, and because I'm Latina and a Spanish speaker, in my first volunteer doula gig at a public hospital in North Carolina, I saw clearly how race and class impacted the experiences of the women that I supported. If we take a look at the statistics about the rates of illness during pregnancy and childbirth, we see clearly the pattern outlined by Dr. Williams. African-American women in particular have an entirely different experience than white women when it comes to whether their babies are born healthy. In certain parts of the country, particularly the Deep South, the rates of mother and infant death for black women actually approximate those rates in sub-Saharan Africa. In those same communities, the rates for white women are near zero. Even nationally, black women are four times more likely to die during pregnancy and childbirth than white women. Four times more likely to die. They're also twice as likely for their infants to die before the first year of life than white infants, and two to three times more likely to give birth too early or too skinny, a sign of insufficient development. Native women are also more likely to have higher rates of these problems than white women, as are some groups of Latinas. For the last decade as a doula-turned-journalist and blogger, I've been trying to raise the alarm about just how different the experiences of women of color, but particularly black women, are when it comes to pregnancy and birth in the US. But when I tell people about these appalling statistics, I'm usually met with an assumption that it's about either poverty or lack of access to care. But it turns out neither of these things tell the whole story. Even middle-class black women still have much worse outcomes than their middle-class white counterparts. The gap actually widens among this group. And while access to care is definitely still a problem, even women of color who receive the recommended prenatal care still suffer from these high rates. And so we come back to the path from discrimination to stress to poor health, and it begins to paint a picture that many people of color know to be true. Racism is actually making us sick. Still sound like a stretch? Consider this. Immigrants, particularly black and Latina immigrants, actually have better health when they first arrive in the United States. But the longer they stay in this country, the worse their health becomes. People like me, born in the United States to Cuban immigrant parents, are actually more likely to have worse health than my grandparents did. It's what researchers call the immigrant paradox, and it further illustrates that there's something in the US environment that is making us sick. But here's the thing. This problem that racism is making people of color, but especially black women and babies sick, is vast. I could spend all of my time with you talking about it, but I won't because I want to make sure to tell you about one solution. And the good news is, it's a solution that isn't particularly expensive, doesn't require any fancy drug treatments or new technologies. The solution is called the JJ way. Meet Jenny Joseph. She's a midwife in the Orlando, Florida, who's been serving pregnant women for over a decade. In what she calls her easy access clinics, Jenny and her team provide prenatal care to over 600 women per year. Her clients, most of whom are black, Haitian and Latina, deliver at the local hospital. But by providing accessible and respectful prenatal care, Jenny has achieved something remarkable. Almost all of her clients give birth to healthy, full-term babies. Her method is deceptively simple. Jenny says that all of her appointments start at the front desk. Every member of her team and every moment a woman is at her clinic is as supportive as possible. No one is turned away due to lack of funds. The JJ way is to make the finances work, no matter what the hurdles. No one is chastised for showing up late to their appointments. No one is talked down to or belittled. Jenny's waiting room feels more like your aunt's living room than a clinic. She calls this space a classroom in disguise. With the plush chairs arranged in a circle, women wait for their appointments in one-on-one chats with a staff educator or in group prenatal classes. When you finally are called back to your appointment, you're greeted by Alexis or Trina, two of Jenny's medical assistants. Both are young, African-American and moms themselves. Their approach is casual and friendly. During one visit, I was asked to tell Jenny that I was going to be a mother. During one visit, I observed Trina chatted with a young, soon-to-be mom while she took her blood pressure. This Latina mom was having trouble keeping food down due to nausea. As Trina deflated the blood pressure cuff, she said, "'We'll see about changing your prescription, OK? We can't have you not eating.'" That we is actually a really crucial aspect of Jenny's model. She sees her staff as part of a team that has one goal, get mom to term with a healthy baby. Jenny says that Trina and Alexis are actually the center of her care model and that her role as a provider is just to support their work. Trina spends a lot of her day on her cell phone, texting with clients about all sorts of things. One woman texted to ask if a medication that she was prescribed at the hospital was OK to take while pregnant. The answer was no. Another woman texted with pictures of an infant born under Jenny's care. Lastly, when you finally are called back to see the provider, you've already taken your own weight in the waiting room and done your own pee test in the bathroom. This is a big departure from the traditional medical model because it places responsibility and information back in the woman's hands. So rather than a medical setting where you might be chastised for not keeping up with provider recommendations, the kind of settings often available to low-income women, Jenny's model is to be as supportive as possible. And that support provides a crucial buffer to the stress of racism and discrimination facing these women every day. But here's the best thing about Jenny's model. It's been incredibly successful. Remember those statistics I told you, that black women are more likely to give birth too early, to give birth to low-birth weight babies, to even die due to complications of pregnancy and childbirth? Well, the JJ way has almost entirely eliminated those problems, starting with what Jenny calls skinny babies. She's been able to get almost all her clients to term with healthy, chunky babies like this one. This is an infant, a baby girl, born to a client of Jenny's this past June. So a similar demographic of women in Jenny's area who gave birth at the same hospital her clients did were three times more likely to give birth to a baby below a healthy weight. Jenny is making headway into what has been seen for decades as an almost intractable problem. So some of you might be thinking, all this one-on-one attention that the JJ way requires must be too expensive to scale. Well, you'd be wrong. The visit with the provider is not the center of Jenny's model, and for good reason. Those visits are expensive, and in order to maintain her model, she's got to see a lot of clients to cover costs. But Jenny doesn't have to spend a ton of time with each woman if all of the members of her team can provide the support, information and care that her clients need. The beauty of Jenny's model is that she actually believes it can be implemented in pretty much any health care setting. It's a revolution in care just waiting to happen. So these problems I've been sharing with you are big. They come from long histories of racism, classism, a society based on race and class stratification. They involve elaborate physiological mechanisms meant to protect us that, when overstimulated, actually make us sick. But if there's one thing I've learned from my work as a doula, it's that a little bit of unconditional support can go a really long way. History has shown that people are incredibly resilient, and while we can't eradicate racism or the stress that results from it overnight, we might just be able to create environments that provide a buffer to what people of color experience on a daily basis. And during pregnancy, that buffer can be an incredible tool towards shifting the impact of racism on generations to come. Thank you. Thank you very much. Thank you.
